intro: The clearest split is that np and React Native overlap on scope but take different approaches. np is CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Release Tooling; React Native is Release / App Store Operations tool for developers. Specializes in Cross Platform Framework. The data points np at automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code, and React Native at mobile app release automation and over-the-air updates. Pricing: np is free, free tier; React Native is free, free tier. Lock-in is low for np and low for React Native, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, np emphasises automates app store submissions and release management, and React Native emphasises automates app store submissions and release management. Neither lists an enterprise tier, so contract-level buying may require a direct conversation. Honest trade-off: neither fits web-only products without native app distribution, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: np is for mobile teams shipping frequent app updates; React Native is for mobile teams shipping frequent app updates; decide based on which scope-fit aligns with your team.
verdict_a: Choose np if your workload matches mobile teams shipping frequent app updates and your work involves automated build/test/deploy pipelines. Skip it if your context is web-only products without native app distribution.
verdict_b: Choose React Native if your workload matches mobile teams shipping frequent app updates and your work involves mobile app release automation. Skip it if your context is web-only products without native app distribution.

faq.q: Is np cheaper than React Native?
faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for np and unknown for React Native. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from np to React Native?
faq.a: np is tagged with low lock-in (oss npm release cli). React Native is tagged with low lock-in (oss cross-platform framework). Both sides are low lock-in, so a migration is feasible but still needs planning around data export and SDK rewrites.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, np or React Native?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is React Native a good alternative to np?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches mobile teams shipping frequent app updates, since both list the same fit. React Native is flagged as not for web-only products without native app distribution, so confirm your context does not fall there.
